title: 第 42 節
en: 12 (32). O Maker of the material world, thou Holy One! Which is the urine wherewith the corpsebearers shall wash their hair and their bodies ? Is it of sheep or of oxen ? Is it of man or of woman ?

13 (35). Ahura Mazda answered : ' It is of sheep or of oxen ; not of man ner of woman, except a man or a woman who has married the next-of-kin ' : these ^ The corpse-bearers or nasu-kasha (Khftndyas). 'The corpse must be carried by two persons (see Faig. Ill, 13 seq.), no matter who they are; they may be a man and a woman, or two women' (Comm.)
' ' As thej are exchanged for the special clothes in which they carry corpses' (Comm.), the 80<aUed ^ftma-i dakhma, ^the Dakhma clothes.'
• The Dakhma (see Farg. VI, 50 seq.)
^ The priest who directs the funerads, * the chief of the Nasukashas ' (Comm.), the so-called Nasfi-s&l&r.

of the good works that Ahriman dreads moat (Shftyaat Id-shAyast XVIII ; West, Pahlavi Texts, 1, 389). < Aharman and the demons are less predominant in the body of him who practises Kh£tAd&d ' (West, II. 422). Therefore their matsma is as powerful as the gdm£a.
FARGARD VIII. 99 shall therefore procure the urine wherewith the corpse*bearers shall wash their hair and their bodies W III.

14.(38). O Maker of the material world, thou Holy One ! Can the way, whereon the carcases of dogs or corpses of men have been carried, be passed through again by flocks and herds, by men and women, by th6 fire of Ahura Mazda, by the consecrated bundles of Baresma, and by the faithful ?

15 (40). Ahura Mazda answered: 'It cannot be passed through again by flocks and herds, nor by men and women, nor by the fire of Ahura Mazda, nor by the consecrated bundles of Baresnia, nor by the faithful >.

16 (41). ' They shall therefore cause a yellow dog with four eyes •, or a white dog with yellow ears, to go three times through that way^. When either the yellow dog with four eyes, or the white dog with yellow ears, is brought there, then the Dn\f Nasu flies away to the regions of the north, [in the shape of a raging fly, with knees and tail sticking out, droning without end, and like unto the foulest Khrafstras*.] ^ 'When back in the village they perform the regular Barashni^m with consecrated gdmfiz' (Comm.)

' The way by which the corpse has passed is haunted by the Drvi^ Nasu : the Dro^ is expelled from it by the same proceeding as it was expelled from the dead, by the Sag-dtd. The Sag-dtd for the purification of the way seems to have fallen into desuetude.
^ 'Afrag sajrs, the dog goes straight along the length of the way; Maidyd-mfih says, he goes across it from side to side' (Comm.)
» Cf.Farg.VII, 3.
H 2 lOO VENDteAD.

1 7 (45). 'If the dog goes unwillingly, O Spitama Zarathu<rt:ra, they shall cause the yellow dog with four eyes, or the white dog with yellow ears, to go six times ^ through that way. When either the yellow dog with four eyes, or the white dog with yellow ears, is brought there, then the Drv^ Nasu flies away to the regions of the north, [in the shape of a raging fly, with knees and tail sticking out, droning without end, and like unto the foulest Khrafstras.] 18 (47). Mf the dog goes unwillingly, they shall cause the yellow dog with four eyes, or the white dog with yellow ears, to go nine times through that way, When either the yellow dog with four eyes, or the white dog with yellow ears, has been brought there, then the Dn\f Nasu flies away to the regions of the north, [in the shape of a raging fly, with knees and tail sticking out, droning without end, and like unto the foulest Khrafstras.] 1 9 (49)- ' An Athravan shall first go along the way and shall say aloud these victorious words: "Yatha aha vairy6«:— The will of the Lord is the law of righteousness.
' " The gifts of Vohu-man6 * to the deeds done in this world for Mazda.
^ < Three times suffice if the dog goes of his own accord ; if he goes by force, it counts as nothing; if he goes but with reluctance, that shall suffice' (Comm. ad § 18).

' A prayer in frequent use, and considered of great efficacy, generally known as the Ahuna Vairya or Honover. It was by reciting it that Ormazd in his first conflict with Ahriman drove him back to hell (Bund. I).
• Of Paradise, as Vohu-man6 (Good Thought) is the doorkeeper of heaven (cf. Farg. XIX, 31), FARGARD VIII. JOT ' •* H e who relieves the poor makes Ahura king.

20(52). *"K^m-n4 mazd4M— What protector hast thou given unto me, O Mazda ! while the hate of the wicked encompasses me ? Whom but thy Atar and Vohu-man6 \ through whose work I keep on the world of righteousness ? Reveal therefore to me thy Religion as thy rule ' !

'"K^ verethrem-^4* : — Who is the victorious who will protect thy teaching ? Make it clear that I am the guide for both worlds. May Sraosha come with Vohu-man6 and help whomsoever thou pleasest, O Mazda !

21 (60). '"Keep us from our hater, O Mazda and Armaiti Spe^ta! Perish, O fiendish Dru^! Perish, O brood of the fiend ! Perish, O creation of the fiend ! Perish, O world of the fiend ! Perish away, O Dru^! Rush away, O Dru^! Perish away, O Dru^ ! Perish away to the regions of the north, never more to give unto death the living world of Righteousness!"
22 (63). *Then the worshippers of Mazda may at their will bring by those ways sheep and oxen, men and women, and Fire, the son of Ahura Mazda, the consecrated bundles of Baresma, and the faithful.
» Yasna XLVI, 7.
'Good Thought') and from thy fire, which in the fire ordeal (Var Ntrang) will show my innocence.

refers to the human incarnation of Sraosha, that is to say, to king Vtftdspa, the victorious protector of the Prophet and his Religion, is applied here to the god Sraosha, as a protector of the soul in its passage from this world to tlie other (Farg. VII, 53).

• The worshippers of Mazda may afterwards * prepare meals with meat and wine in that house; it shall be clean, and there will be no sin, as before/ IV.
23 (65). O Maker of the material world, thou Holy One ! If a man shall throw clothes, either of skin or woven, upon a dead body *, enough to cover the feet, what is the penalty that he shall pay ' ?

Ahura Mazda answered : ' Four hundred stripes with the Aspah6-a5tra, four hundred stripes with the Sraosh6-/&arana/ 24 (68). O Maker of the material world, thou Holy One ! If a man shall throw clothes, either of skin or woven, upon a dead body, enough to cover both legs, what is the penalty that he shall pay?
zh: 12 (32). 物質世界的造物者啊，至聖者！屍體搬運者應當用何物的尿液來洗淨他們的頭髮和身體？是綿羊的尿液，抑或牛的尿液？是男人的尿液，抑或女人的尿液？
13 (35). 阿胡拉‧馬茲達答道：「這是關於羊或牛；不是關於任何人的方式，除非是與至親通婚的男人或女人」：這些^ 屍體搬運者或那蘇‧卡沙（Khftndyas）。「屍體必須由兩個人搬運（見 Faig. III, 13 seq.），無論他們是誰；他們可以是男人和女人，或兩個女人」（注疏）
'因為他們要換上用來運送屍體的特殊衣服'（注疏），所謂的「阿特馬—達赫瑪」（*atma-i dakhma*），「達赫瑪衣服」。
• 達克瑪（Dakhma）（見《法爾迦德》VI, 50 及以下）
^ 主持葬禮的祭司，* 那蘇卡沙人（Nasukashas）的首領（注疏），所謂的那蘇飛—薩拉爾（Nasfi-sâlâr）。
關於善行乃是阿赫里曼最為畏懼的事物（Shftyaat Id-shAyast XVIII；West, Pahlavi Texts, 1, 389）。<「阿赫里曼與眾魔在修習凱塔德的人之身體中的優勢較弱」（West, II. 422）。因此他們的matsma與gdm£a一樣強大。
《法爾加德第八卷》第99節。我因此應取得尿液，使得運屍者得以之洗淨他們的頭髮及身體。《西方第三卷》。
14.(38). 物質世界的造物者啊，至聖者！狗的屍體或人的遺骸曾經經過之路，能否再被畜群與牲畜、男人與女人、阿胡拉‧瑪茲達（Ahura Mazda）的聖火、經過祝聖的巴瑞斯瑪（Baresma）束，以及信徒們所通行？
15 (40). 阿胡拉‧瑪茲達回答說：『無論羊群與牲畜，無論男人與女人，無論阿胡拉‧瑪茲達之火，無論巴雷斯尼亞（Baresnia）的聖束，無論信仰者都不能再度通過它。
16 (41). 「因此他們應當令一隻黃色四眼狗，或一隻白色黃耳狗，三次通過該道路。當黃色四眼狗或白色黃耳狗被帶到那裡時，死屍汙穢（Druj Nasu）便以狂躁蒼蠅的形狀飛往北方諸域，[膝蓋與尾巴突出，發出無盡的嗡鳴聲，宛如最邪惡的惡物（Khrafstras）。]^ 「當返回村落時，他們以祝聖的牛尿（gômêz）進行常規的潔淨儀式（Barashnum）」（評註）
屍體所經過的道路被德鲁維·納蘇（Drvi^ Nasu）所纏擾：德鲁維（Dro^）從中被驅逐的方式，與它從死者身上被驅逐的方式相同，即藉由薩格-德塔（Sag-dtd）。用於淨化道路的薩格-德塔似乎已經落入廢棄之地。
^ 「'Afrag sajrs，那隻狗筆直地沿著路的整個長度行進；Maidyd-mfih 說，牠從一邊橫越到另一邊」（註釋）
» 參閱《法爾加德》第七篇第三節。

Once you share the legible source material, I'll be happy to proceed with the translation following all the guidelines you've specified.

17 (45). 「若犬不願前行，聖祆教先知瑣羅亞斯德啊，彼等應令黃色四眼之犬，或白色黃耳之犬，六度經由彼途。當黃色四眼之犬，或白色黃耳之犬，被帶至彼處時，則屍臭惡魔（Drv Nasu）飛往北方諸域，[化為狂怒之蠅形，膝與尾突出，無止境地嗡鳴，宛如最污穢之惡物（Khrafstras）。]」18 (47). 「若犬不願前行，彼等應令黃色四眼之犬，或白色黃耳之犬，九度經由彼途。當黃色四眼之犬，或白色黃耳之犬，被帶至彼處時，則屍臭惡魔（Drv Nasu）飛往北方諸域，[化為狂怒之蠅形，膝與尾突出，無止境地嗡鳴，宛如最污穢之惡物（Khrafstras）。]」19 (49). 「一位祆教祭司應首先沿途前行，並大聲說出以下勝利之言：『Yatha aha vairyō——主之意志乃正義之律。』
「沃胡‧曼（Vohu-man）的恩賜用於此世為瑪茲達所行之事。
^ < 「若狗自願前往，三次足矣；若強行驅使，則不計其數；若狗勉強前往，則一次足矣」（《疏》§ 18）。
一段經常使用且被認為具有極大功效的禱文，通常稱為阿胡那‧瓦伊利亞（Ahuna Vairya）或荷諾沃（Honover）。據說，正是通過誦唸它，歐瑪茲德在他與阿赫里曼的首次衝突中將其驅趕回地獄（Bund. I）。
• 關於樂園，如同沃胡‧曼那（Vohu-man，善念）是天堂的守門人（參照《法爾加德》第十九章第三十一節），《法爾加德》第八章。「施濟貧苦者使阿胡拉為王。」
20(52). *「慈善之主啊，汝予我何保護者，而惡人之恨意環繞於我？除却汝之阿塔爾（Atar）與善思（Vohu-manah）外，尚有誰乎？藉其功業，我得以守持義行之世界。故願汝將汝之宗教啟示於我，作為汝之法則乎！」*
「克雷韋瑞賽姆（Kyθ verethrem）：誰是那勝利者，將保護你的教義？顯示我就是兩個世界的嚮導。願斯勞沙（Sraosha）帶著沃胡-曼（Vohu-man）前來，幫助你所悅納者，啊，瑪茲達！
「願我們遠離仇敵，唯智慧之主馬茲達與聖義之靈阿爾瑪提（Mazda, Armaiti Spęta）！願邪惡之德魯滅亡（Dru）！願邪惡之種滅亡！願邪惡之造物滅亡！願邪惡之世界滅亡！願德魯消逝！願德魯遁去！願德魯消逝！願德魯消逝至北方之地，永不再令義之生活世界陷於死亡！」
22 (63). *那麼，天智神（Mazda）的崇拜者可以依其意願，經由那些途徑帶來羊與牛、男人與女人，以及火——智慧主（Ahura Mazda）之子、被祝聖的巴雷斯瑪聖束（Baresma），以及信徒。
» 瑜伽經（Yasna）第四十六卷第七節。
「善念」）以及來自你的火焰，在火審判儀式（Var Ntrang）中將證明我的無辜。
指涉 Sraosha 的人類化身，亦即指勝利的先知保護者及其宗教的守護者維什塔斯帕王，此處則應用於提婆 Sraosha，作為靈魂從此世往彼世之途中的保護者（《法爾迦德》第七章第五十三節）。
我注意到您提供的「英文原文」似乎不是完整的可翻譯文本，而是零散的字元組合（"M02 . ; ' • • VENdIdAd."）。 請提供完整的英文段落原文，我將按照您的規則進行翻譯。
• 瑪茲達的信仰者日後可在那座殿堂中用肉類與葡萄酒準備祭餐；它將保持潔淨，不會有罪孽，如同往昔一般。IV.
23 (65)。物質世界的造物主，聖潔者啊！如果一個人把衣物，無論是獸皮製或織布製，拋在死屍之上*，足以蓋住雙腳，他應當付出什麼樣的懲罰？
阿胡拉‧馬茲達回答說：「用阿斯帕赫-瓦特拉（Aspah6-a5tra）鞭打四百下，用斯拉歐什-瓦拉納（Sraosh6-/&arana/）鞭打四百下 24 (68)。物質世界的造物主啊，聖聖者！若有人將衣物，無論是獸皮製或編織布料，置於死體之上，足以覆蓋雙腿，他應當付出何等懲罰？
